completed_steps.summary: Classified Lens.Core.Src as a monolith source project with one primary toolkit part
Identified existing deep module documentation under _bmad/lens-work/docs and adapter guidance under .github
Documented the source stack as YAML, Markdown, Python, Node.js, GitHub Actions, and Git
Skipped API, database, and UI-specific artifact generation because the project is a toolkit source module rather than a service or frontend
Generated parent-level source tree analysis for the source wrapper and module core
Captured contributor workflow, validation commands, and source-to-release operating model
Generated a parent-level architecture document for Lens.Core.Src
Wrote overview, component inventory, development guide, and deployment guide

project_parts.role: Authoritative source for the lens-work module and checked-in release adapter assets

project_classification.summary: Lens.Core.Src is a monolith source project that publishes a BMAD lifecycle toolkit rather than an HTTP service or UI application.

existing_documentation.summary: Deep module documentation already exists under _bmad/lens-work/docs, while this scan adds a parent-level map for the source wrapper.

technology_stack.summary: YAML contracts, Markdown documentation and prompts, Python operational scripts, Node.js packaging, GitHub Actions promotion, and Git-based state.

batches_completed.summary: Reviewed checked-in agent, prompt, skill, and workflow sources that define the release-facing adapter layer.
Reviewed the config bridge and module root to capture manifests, lifecycle contracts, skills, scripts, and installer structure.
Reviewed the existing embedded documentation set to avoid duplicating deeper module references at the parent level.

development.summary: Contributors work in Lens.Core.Src, validate targeted changes with uv/pytest or script dry runs, then rely on the promotion workflow and downstream preflight.

deployment.summary: Promotion packages the source project into Lens.Core.Release and requires a downstream pull plus workspace preflight after success.

resume_instructions: Documentation generation is complete. Re-run as a full rescan if the parent source tree or release packaging contract changes materially.
